2605.04308.title: Memory as a Markov Matrix: Sample Efficient Knowledge Expansion via Token-to-Dictionary Mapping
2605.04308.paper_summary: Continual incorporation of new knowledge is essential for the long-term evolution of large language models (LLMs). Existing approaches typically rely on parameter-update algorithms to mitigate catastrophic forgetting, yet they suffer from fundamental limitations: 1) forgetting is unavoidable as the amount of newly injected knowledge grows; and 2) model updates are often irreversible. As modern LLMs become increasingly expressive, it is natural to question whether large-scale weight updates are necessary for acquiring a small amount of new knowledge. In this work, we propose a principled framework that models autoregressive language generation as a Markov process over tokens, where model memory is represented by a Markov transition matrix. Under this formulation, incorporating new knowledge/tokens corresponds to extending the state space, and preserving existing transitions guarantees retention of previously learned knowledge. We then prove a sample complexity bound for incorporating new tokens via a token-to-dictionary mapping strategy. In particular, for learning the transition behavior of each new token, the required number of samples scales linearly with the number of existing tokens it is mapped to. To realize this mapping, we propose an embedding-tuning algorithm that requires minimal parameter updates and induces zero forgetting. Experimental results further demonstrate the effectiveness of our method and validate our theoretical findings.
2605.04308.paper_summary_zh: 持續納入新知識對於大型語言模型（LLMs）的長期演變至關重要。現有的方法通常依賴於參數更新算法來減輕災難性遺忘，但它們存在根本性的限制：1）隨著新注入知識的增長，遺忘是不可避免的；2）模型更新往往是不可逆的。隨著現代LLMs變得越來越具表達力，自然會質疑大規模權重更新是否對於獲取少量新知識是必要的。在這項工作中，我們提出了一個原則性框架，將自回歸語言生成建模為一個基於標記的馬爾可夫過程，其中模型記憶由馬爾可夫轉移矩陣表示。在這種表述下，納入新知識/標記對應於擴展狀態空間，而保留現有轉移則保證了之前學習知識的保留。然後，我們證明了通過標記到字典的映射策略納入新標記的樣本複雜度界限。特別是，對於學習每個新標記的轉移行為，所需的樣本數量與其映射到的現有標記數量呈線性增長。為了實現這一映射，我們提出了一種嵌入調整算法，該算法需要最小的參數更新並且不會引起遺忘。實驗結果進一步證明了我們方法的有效性並驗證了我們的理論發現。
2605.04308.author: Kaustubh Pethkar et.al.
2605.04308.authors: Kaustubh Pethkar, Ziyang Xiong, Zuofeng Shang, Yingcong Li
